extra.message: User input from request.args.get('name') (sandboxes/views.py:13) is passed to the template and rendered with the |safe filter, which disables Jinja2 auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the 'name' GET parameter, e.g. ?name=<script>alert(1)</script>, achieving reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from request.form.get('name') or request.cookies.get('name') (sandboxes/views.py:28-34) is stored in a cookie (line 38) and rendered with |safe, disabling Jinja2 auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the 'name' form field or cookie, achieving stored XSS that persists across page reloads.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The message.from_user field is rendered with |safe in the admin message panel. The from_user field is user-controlled via the unauthenticated API endpoint MessagesView.post() (api/views.py:50) which sets from_user from request JSON data. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript into from_user without authentication, achieving stored XSS that executes in the admin's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The message.message field is rendered with |safe in the admin message panel. The message content is user-controlled via the unauthenticated API endpoint MessagesView.post() (api/views.py:47-52) and the authenticated send_feedback endpoint (account_area/views.py:66-76). An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript into message content without authentication, achieving stored XSS that executes in the admin's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The logged-in user's username is rendered with |safe in the navigation bar on every page. If a user's username contains HTML/JavaScript (e.g., set via the admin panel's User CRUD interface at flask_xss.py:50), it will execute as stored XSS across all pages for any user viewing the navbar. Jinja2 auto-escaping is explicitly disabled by |safe.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The 'next' parameter from request.args is used directly in redirect() without validation. An attacker can craft a URL like /toggle_xss_protection/?next=https://evil.com to redirect users to an arbitrary external site. The base template (_base.html:66-67) also passes g.path (request.path) as the next parameter, but an attacker can override this via the query string.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'Keep it secret, keep it safe'. This key is used for signing session cookies and CSRF tokens. A hardcoded, guessable secret key allows an attacker to forge session cookies, potentially gaining unauthorized access as any user including admin.

User passwords are stored in plaintext in the database (db.Column(db.String(200)) without any hashing). The login view (login/views.py:17) compares passwords using direct string equality: `user.password == password`. The change password endpoint (account_area/views.py:35) stores the new password as plaintext. This means anyone with database access can read all user passwords.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The User.serialize property includes the plaintext password field. This property is used by the UsersView.index() API endpoint (api/views.py:72) which returns all users including their passwords in JSON responses. This exposes plaintext passwords to any admin user accessing the API, and could also be exposed through error messages or logs.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: The MessagesView.post() method lacks the @admin_required decorator that protects all other methods in this class (index, get, delete, acknowledge_message). This allows any unauthenticated user to create messages with arbitrary content (including malicious HTML/JavaScript) that are rendered with |safe in the admin panel, enabling unauthenticated stored XSS attacks against admins.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Flask DEBUG mode is enabled (app.config['DEBUG'] = True). In production, this exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows arbitrary Python code execution from the browser. Combined with the hardcoded SECRET_KEY, this creates a critical remote code execution risk.

The application actively disables the browser's XSS protection mechanism by setting the X-XSS-Protection header to 0 when session-based XSS protection is not enabled. This removes a defense-in-depth layer that could mitigate some reflected XSS attacks, making the existing XSS vulnerabilities easier to exploit.
